title: New Activity at Possible Chinese Intelligence Facilities in Cuba
gist: New satellite imagery shows a large new signals-intelligence antenna array has become operational at Bejucal, Cuba, near Havana, while a second suspected SIGINT site at El Salao remains largely stalled; the facilities, likely linked to China, could monitor sensitive U.S. activity 90 miles offshore and become a flashpoint as the Trump administration pressures Havana.

themes.theme: Operational new SIGINT array at Bejucal

insights.claim: Construction of a new large circularly disposed antenna array at the Bejucal SIGINT site near Havana, Cuba, now appears complete and the facility has very likely begun operations.
insights.speaker: CSIS
insights.so_what: A newly live foreign SIGINT capability near U.S. shores raises the odds of escalation in U.S.-Cuba and U.S.-China tensions.
insights.quote: Construction now appears to be complete and the facility has very likely begun operations.
insights.locator: Over the last two years, an antenna field

insights.claim: The Bejucal array of 32 antennas (19 outer and 13 inner) is larger and likely more capable than any Cuban CDAA previously observed by CSIS, and is used for high-frequency direction finding to intercept and geolocate radio transmissions.
insights.speaker: CSIS
insights.so_what: Indicates a step-change in collection capability that intelligence and defense observers will track closely.
insights.quote: The array of 32 antennas (19 outer and 13 inner) is larger and likely more capable than any Cuban CDAA previously observed by CSIS.

insights.claim: From its northwest Cuba location, the Bejucal array could improve the ability of Cuban authorities or their foreign partners to monitor sensitive U.S. naval and air operations across the Caribbean, the Gulf of Mexico, and the southeastern seaboard.
insights.speaker: CSIS
insights.so_what: Heightened surveillance of U.S. military movements could constrain regional operations and sharpen the security debate.
insights.quote: the CDAA could improve the ability of Cuban authorities—or potentially their foreign partners—to monitor sensitive U.S. activities in the Caribbean and across the southeastern seaboard

insights.claim: The Bejucal facility has long been associated with China and is likely one of three sites in Cuba that U.S. officials have recently acknowledged is operated by China, though no clear public evidence proves China's involvement there.
insights.speaker: CSIS
insights.so_what: Confirmed Chinese operation near U.S. territory would be a material escalation in great-power intelligence competition.
insights.quote: Bejucal is likely one of the three sites in Cuba that U.S. officials have recently acknowledged is operated by China
insights.locator: The Bejucal facility has long been associated

insights.claim: Senior U.S. officials have recently warned about expanding Chinese and Russian intelligence activities in Cuba, located just 90 miles from U.S. shores.
insights.speaker: CSIS
insights.so_what: Rising official attention signals potential for sanctions, diplomatic pressure, or military responses near U.S. waters.
insights.quote: Recent warnings from senior U.S. officials about expanding Chinese and Russian intelligence activities have once again drawn attention to Cuba

insights.claim: At the suspected CDAA site at El Salao, first uncovered by CSIS in 2024, progress has slowed nearly to a halt, with no antennas installed and no signs the site is operational as of May 2026 imagery.
insights.speaker: CSIS
insights.so_what: Uneven buildout suggests resource or strategic constraints; the pace of work is a leading indicator of intent.
insights.quote: No antennas have been installed and no apparent changes have been made to the surrounding buildings that would indicate that the site is operational.
insights.locator: Little has changed in the imagery

insights.claim: A repaved access road repositioned toward the center of the El Salao array suggests the site has not been fully abandoned, though it may be completed as planned or repurposed for another use.
insights.speaker: CSIS
insights.so_what: Ambiguous activity warrants continued monitoring before drawing conclusions about the site's fate.
insights.quote: it would be unusual to establish a paved road through the center of an active antenna array
insights.locator: Still, it appears that work there

insights.claim: If completed, El Salao could complement Bejucal by extending geographic coverage over the Southeast Caribbean, letting the two facilities triangulate signals across Central America and the Western Atlantic.
insights.speaker: CSIS
insights.so_what: A networked two-site capability would significantly expand the collection footprint near U.S. interests.
insights.quote: Working in concert, the two facilities would further enhance Cuba’s ability to triangulate signals emanating across Central America and the Western Atlantic region.

themes.theme: Geopolitical flashpoint with the Trump administration

insights.claim: The continued expansion of the Cuban intelligence sites may become a flashpoint as the Trump administration accelerates pressure on the Cuban regime, including a May 2026 executive order imposing new sanctions citing Cuba's hosting of foreign adversary facilities.
insights.speaker: CSIS
insights.so_what: Sanctions trajectory and negotiation posture on Cuba are tied to these sites; watch for further escalation.
insights.quote: the White House cited Cuba’s hosting of “foreign adversary facilities” targeting sensitive U.S. national security information as evidence of “malign influence.”
insights.locator: The continued expansion of these sites

insights.claim: The status of the Cuban intelligence sites could be a key part of any future negotiations between Washington and Havana about Cuba's future.
insights.speaker: CSIS
insights.so_what: These facilities become bargaining chips, linking intelligence developments to diplomatic and sanctions outcomes.
insights.quote: The status of these sites could be a key part of any negotiations between Washington and Havana about Cuba’s future.
insights.locator: The continued expansion of these sites
